title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Hakast meets Valerie Brand, the wife of Josea Brand. Valerie is described as having a pale complexion and a weak, melancholic voice, appearing to enjoy her state of illness. Hakast shows her a photo of the murder victim, an insurance agent. Valerie expresses curiosity but incorrectly assumes the man was strangled; Hakast clarifies he was stabbed. Valerie claims she does not know the man and asks if he had any connection to their neighbor, Miss Pembroke, but Hakast states Miss Pembroke did not know him.

Regarding the neighborhood, Josea Brand explains that while he is not a real gardener, he employs Mr. McNaughton from number 63 to maintain their garden. When questioned about the time of the murder, between one and three o'clock, Josea states that he and Valerie were having lunch together around one or one-thirty. He notes that their dining room window faces the street, so they could not see the garden, and that Valerie usually rests upstairs after lunch. Josea reveals that the couple's lifestyle improved a year ago after Valerie inherited money from an uncle she had not seen in twenty-five years. He expresses interest in traveling to Greece, though Valerie prefers to stay in England due to her health and social connections.

After leaving the Brands, Hakast and Coling discuss the case. Meanwhile, at 62 Wilbraham Crescent, Mrs. Rymes deals with a commotion in her kitchen caused by her sons, Bill and Ted, who were using jars as bowling balls. The boys engage in a fierce wrestling match, prompting Mrs. Rymes to push them out of the kitchen so she can clean up the broken ceramics.
